# Renzo Ramos - Backend-Oriented Full Stack Developer

> Renzo Ramos is a backend-oriented Full Stack Developer based in Madrid, Spain. He specializes in Java and Spring Boot, building scalable and maintainable REST APIs applying clean architecture, SOLID principles, and domain-driven design. Currently employed at SEIDOR as a Fullstack Developer. Bridges backend logic and frontend interface using React and TypeScript. Integrates AI tooling deliberately into his development workflow as a force multiplier.

## Key Facts

- **Portfolio**: https://renzoramosdev.github.io/RenzoRamosPortafolio/
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/RenzoRamosDEV
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/renzoinv04/
- **Email**: renzoramosivan@gmail.com
- **Location**: Madrid, Spain
- **Specialization**: Java, Spring Boot, REST API design, microservices, Docker

## Work Experience

### SEIDOR — Fullstack Developer | September 2025 – Present | Madrid, Spain

Develops reusable and configurable components in Adobe Experience Manager (AEM). Implements backend modal logic in Java. Manages and publishes multimedia content via AEM Assets (DAM). Resolves backend incidents, optimizes existing functionality, and produces technical documentation for project support and maintenance.

## Education

### CESUR — Higher Vocational Training in Cross-Platform Application Development | September 2024 – June 2026 | Madrid, Spain

Focused on Java, Spring Boot, Kotlin, Python, HTML, CSS, Maven, and Gradle. Covers backend and frontend application development, REST APIs, dependency management, web interfaces, and good programming practices.

### 42 Madrid — Fundación Telefónica Bootcamp | July 2025 – August 2025 | Madrid, Spain

Intensive bootcamp focused on C, Linux, and shell scripting. Peer-to-peer learning methodology with student review and collaborative validation.

### Instituto San Ignacio de Loyola — Graphic Design | 2021 – 2022 | Lima, Peru

Training in visual communication and design tools: Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, and InDesign. Provides a design sensibility that informs UI decisions in frontend work.

## Tech Stack

### Backend

- **Languages**: Java, Kotlin
- **Frameworks**: Spring Boot, Spring Modulith
- **Database**: MySQL (relational design, complex queries, referential integrity)
- **Containers**: Docker, Docker Compose (reproducible environments, shared networks, healthchecks)
- **Build tools**: Maven, Gradle
- **Testing**: JUnit, Mockito, Testcontainers (unit, integration, and TDD)
- **API documentation**: Swagger / OpenAPI via SpringDoc

### Frontend

- **Languages**: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript
- **Library**: React (component-based UIs, state management)
- **Styling**: TailwindCSS, CSS Modules

### AI Tooling

Uses AI tooling deliberately: Claude Code during refactoring and architecture decisions, Copilot CLI for terminal-based suggestions, and OpenSpec for drafting technical specs before implementation. Not as a crutch — as a force multiplier.

### Version Control

- **Git**: Branching strategy (Git Flow / Trunk-Based), structured commit discipline, team collaboration

## Methodologies

### Architecture
- SOLID Principles — maintainable, decoupled, testable code
- Clean Architecture — separation of business logic from technical details

### Testing
- TDD (Test-Driven Development) — design through tests before implementation
- Integration Testing — module and service integration validation
- Testcontainers — tests with real databases in controlled environments

### Process
- Agile (Scrum / Kanban) — iterative value delivery and continuous improvement
- Spec-Driven Development — clear technical contracts before development
- Agentic Workflows — automated development and debugging with intelligent agents
- Prompt Engineering — AI interaction optimization

### UI
- Component-Based UI — modular, scalable, reusable interfaces
- Responsive Design — mobile-first, adapts to any device and resolution

## Projects

### 01 — Booqi: Event Booking Management System

- **Repository**: https://github.com/RenzoRamosDEV/Booqui
- **Type**: Team project — backend ownership across all 4 microservices (users, events, bookings, payments); sole responsible for Docker Compose orchestration (shared network, healthchecks, environment configuration). Frontend developed by teammates.
- **Architecture**: 4 independent Spring Boot microservices, each with its own MySQL database and Swagger/OpenAPI-documented REST API
- **Inter-service communication**: Real-time validation of user identity, event existence, and seat availability across services
- **Key features**: Centralized ticket purchasing, PDF ticket generation on booking confirmation, admin panel for organizers (event creation, booking and payment monitoring), real-time availability control
- **Technologies**: Java, Spring Boot, SpringDoc, Maven, MySQL, React, Bootstrap, TypeScript, Docker

### 02 — Product Inventory Management System

- **Repository**: https://github.com/RenzoRamosDEV/Gestion-De-Inventario
- **Type**: Sole developer — full-stack, from database design to frontend SPA
- **Backend**: Java 17, Spring Boot, layered architecture, DTO mapping with MapStruct, centralized exception handling, pagination, multi-criteria search filters, Spring Boot Actuator for monitoring, Swagger/OpenAPI documentation
- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript SPA with real-time dashboard (total stock value, low-stock alerts, out-of-stock count, most expensive products), strict end-to-end TypeScript typing across all API communication
- **Key features**: Soft deletion with record recovery, real-time stock adjustment from the main table, multi-criteria filtering by name, stock status, and deleted state
- **Technologies**: Java 17, Spring Boot, SpringDoc, Maven, MySQL, React, TypeScript, TailwindCSS
